answers.verdict: Out of scope — truss (multiple connected bodies)
answers.reason: Finding member forces requires analyzing each joint separately (method of joints) or cutting the truss (method of sections). This is a multi-body problem, not a single rigid body problem.

solution_steps: Identify problem type: truss = multiple two-force members connected at joints.
External reactions at A and D CAN be found with 3 equilibrium equations (ΣFx, ΣFy, ΣM).
Member forces require method of joints or sections — these are internal forces of the truss system.
This problem is out of scope for a single rigid body solver.

The tutor should redirect: 'This is a truss problem (multiple connected members). Our scope is single rigid bodies. I can help you set up the external reactions, but member force analysis is beyond our current scope.'
